no_solution_leakage.reason: The instruction is extremely prescriptive: it specifies the exact variable name 'killAll', exact description string, exact file paths, exact function behavior, exact documentation format including the exact comment string '# forcefully kill all daemon processes (for stale/zombie processes)'. This is more of a step-by-step implementation guide than a problem description.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests assert on gold-specific implementation details: test_kill_all_command_declared checks for exact string 'const killAll = declareCommand', test_kill_all_implemented_in_program checks for exact function name 'killAllDaemons', test_skill_md_follows_existing_format checks the exact format 'playwright-cli kill-all'. tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Almost all tests are text-matching: they read file contents and assert substrings. test_typescript_compiles does run tsc compilation, but the remaining 7 tests are purely 'assert X in open(path).read()' pattern with no behavioral execution of the kill-all command.

test_not_tautological.reason: A stub/empty implementation would not pass because tests check for specific strings in specific files (e.g., 'const killAll = declareCommand' in commands.ts, 'killAllDaemons' function in program.ts, 'kill-all' in SKILL.md). These require actual code/doc additions. The TypeScript compilation test also ensures syntactic correctness.

anti_cheating_measures.reason: The solution file exists at /workspace/playwright and solve.sh is in /solution/. An agent could read /solution/solve.sh or the patch content to extract the exact implementation. Additionally, since tests are purely text-matching, the agent could simply write the exact expected strings into the files.

behavior_in_tests.reason: The instruction requires the function to 'report how many processes were killed' and 'gracefully handle cases where no processes are found', but no test verifies this runtime behavior. Tests only check static text patterns in source files.
